---
title: "WMTA"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#going to do a year comparison of the daily ridership in metro riders before and after Trump's election
#Going to do some basic filtering to try and determine patterns 

daily_rider <- read.csv("data/daily_rider_data.csv") %>% 
  clean_names()
colnames(daily_rider) <- as.character(unlist(daily_rider[1, ]))  # Set first row as column names
daily_rider <- daily_rider[-1, ]

```

```{r}
#filter for only weekdays because those are the most likely days that people are going into work 
colnames(daily_rider) <- tolower(gsub(" ", "_", colnames(daily_rider)))
daily_rider_week <- daily_rider%>% 
  filter(`weekday_/_saturday_/_sunday`=="Weekday")

workweek_daily_rider <- daily_rider_week %>%
  filter(holiday == "No" | date == "January 20, 2025")  


```

```{r}
#Now I will be separating it to after Trump's innauguration and before

workweek_daily_rider <- workweek_daily_rider %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date, format = "%B %d, %Y"))  # Adjust format if needed

trump_daily_rider <- workweek_daily_rider %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2025-01-20") & date <= as.Date("2025-02-18"))

```

Now that I have multiple working data-sets with the information I need I can begin making bar charts and making an analysis.

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(trump_daily_rider, aes(x = date, y = grand_total)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  scale_x_date(date_breaks = "1 day", date_labels = "%b %d") +  # Ensures correct date scale
  labs(title = "Grand Total Over Time",
       x = "Date",
       y = "Grand Total") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))  


```

```{r}
write.csv(workweek_daily_rider,"~/Downloads/workweek_daily_rider.csv", row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(daily_rider_week, "~/Downloads/workweek_daily_rider.csv",row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
workweek_daily_rider$date <- as.Date(workweek_daily_rider$date, format = "%Y-%m-%d")
filtered_data <- workweek_daily_rider %>%
  filter(date >= as.Date("2020-01-01") & date <= as.Date("2025-12-31"))

```

```{r}
workweek_daily_rider$rail <- as.numeric(gsub(",", "", workweek_daily_rider$rail))  # Remove commas
workweek_daily_rider$rail <- as.numeric(trimws(workweek_daily_rider$rail))  # Trim spaces
str(workweek_daily_rider)  # Check data types of all columns


```
